cards.definition: The latest time by which something must be finished.
cards.example: The report is due by Friday's deadline.

cards.definition: To arrange for something to happen at a fixed time; a plan of times.
cards.example: The staff meeting is scheduled for 9 A.M.

cards.definition: To delay something until a later time.
cards.example: The seminar was postponed until next Thursday.

cards.definition: A list of items to be discussed at a meeting.
cards.example: Please review the agenda before the conference call.

cards.definition: The official written record of what was said at a meeting.
cards.example: She took the minutes during the board meeting.

cards.definition: To include a file with an e-mail or document.
cards.example: I have attached the revised proposal to this e-mail.

cards.definition: To state or verify that something is certain.
cards.example: Please confirm your attendance by Monday.

cards.definition: To help someone remember something.
cards.example: Remind the team about the training session tomorrow.

cards.definition: A person you work with.
cards.example: My colleague will cover my shift on Saturday.

cards.definition: A person who oversees and directs the work of others.
cards.example: Report any equipment problems to your supervisor.

cards.definition: A piece of work that must be done.
cards.example: Each task must be completed before the deadline.

cards.definition: Working well without wasting time or resources.
cards.example: The new filing process is more efficient than the old one.

cards.definition: To officially agree to or accept something.
cards.example: The manager approved the budget request.

cards.definition: To hand in a document for review or a decision.
cards.example: Submit your expense report by the fifth of the month.

cards.definition: To make a meeting or process easier to complete.
cards.example: The coordinator will facilitate the planning session.

cards.definition: To organize people or activities so they work together.
cards.example: Please coordinate the room setup with Facilities.

cards.definition: To send information to several people.
cards.example: The agenda was circulated before the meeting.

cards.definition: A short written message used in a workplace.
cards.example: The memo explains the updated travel policy.

cards.definition: A short meeting that gives important information.
cards.example: The morning briefing starts at 8:30.

cards.definition: To give a task or responsibility to another person.
cards.example: The manager delegated the inventory check to Kim.

cards.definition: To collect information into one document or list.
cards.example: She compiled the survey results into a report.

cards.definition: An early version of a document; to write one.
cards.example: Send me the draft before it goes to Legal.

cards.definition: To change and improve written work or a plan.
cards.example: The team revised the proposal after the client call.

cards.definition: A telephone number inside an organization.
cards.example: Call payroll at extension 2280.

cards.definition: An area where someone works.
cards.example: The new workspace has adjustable desks.

cards.definition: A small separated work area in an office.
cards.example: Visitors should check in before entering the cubicle area.

cards.definition: Paper, envelopes, and other office writing supplies.
cards.example: Order stationery through the purchasing portal.

cards.definition: A paper copy made with a copier; to make such a copy.
cards.example: Please photocopy the handout for all attendees.

cards.definition: A place where old records are stored; to store records.
cards.example: The contract is kept in the digital archive.

cards.definition: Private and not meant to be shared widely.
cards.example: Confidential files must be stored securely.

cards.definition: A short message telling someone about something important.
cards.example: Please check your email notifications for meeting updates.

cards.definition: A book giving information such as rules or instructions about a job or organization.
cards.example: New employees receive a copy of the staff handbook on their first day.

cards.definition: The person responsible for writing the official record of a meeting.
cards.example: She serves as the minutes-taker for all board meetings.

cards.definition: A piece of office furniture with drawers and shelves for storing documents.
cards.example: The extra printer paper is in the supply cabinet by the copier.

cards.definition: A system that protects a computer network from unauthorized access.
cards.example: The company's firewall blocked several suspicious emails last month.

cards.definition: The practice of working from home using a computer and telephone.
cards.example: Many employees request telework one or two days per week.

cards.definition: Describes being away from work, especially on leave or travel.
cards.example: I am out-of-office until Monday and will reply to your email then.

cards.definition: To collect and arrange information or pages in the correct order.
cards.example: Please collate the reports before the meeting.

cards.definition: A bill listing goods or services and the amount owed.
cards.example: The invoice must be paid within thirty days of receipt.

cards.definition: To pay someone back for money they have spent or lost.
cards.example: The store will reimburse customers for damaged items.

cards.definition: Money spent on something, especially for business.
cards.example: Travel expenses are reviewed before payments are issued.

cards.definition: A plan for how money will be spent.
cards.example: The marketing budget was increased this quarter.

cards.definition: An approximate calculation of cost or value.
cards.example: The repair total was lower than the original estimate.

cards.definition: Money returned to a customer; to return that money.
cards.example: Cancellations made a week in advance receive a full refund.

cards.definition: A reduction in the usual price.
cards.example: Orders of ten bags or more receive a 10 percent discount.

cards.definition: The income a business receives from its activities.
cards.example: Online sales now account for half of the company's revenue.

cards.definition: The money gained after all costs are paid.
cards.example: The company reported a record profit this year.

cards.definition: Happening once every three months.
cards.example: The accounting team prepares quarterly reports.

cards.definition: A single instance of buying or selling.
cards.example: Each transaction appears on your monthly statement.

cards.definition: A record of money held or owed; a customer relationship.
cards.example: The coordinator responds to account inquiries.

cards.definition: An official inspection of financial records.
cards.example: The audit team reviewed every inventory record.

cards.definition: One of several scheduled partial payments.
cards.example: The equipment can be paid for in six monthly installments.

cards.definition: The amount of money remaining in an account or owed.
cards.example: The unpaid balance is due by Friday.

cards.definition: To subtract an amount from a total.
cards.example: The coupon value will be deducted at checkout.

cards.definition: Money paid in advance; to place money in an account.
cards.example: A 25 percent deposit is required to reserve the room.

cards.definition: Not paid or completed by the required date.
cards.example: Overdue invoices may include a service charge.

cards.definition: Proof that money was paid or goods were received.
cards.example: Attach the receipt to your expense report.

cards.definition: A document showing account activity.
cards.example: The monthly statement lists every transaction.

cards.definition: To assign money or resources for a purpose.
cards.example: Funds were allocated for new safety equipment.

cards.definition: An amount that is more than needed.
cards.example: The surplus will be used for staff training.

cards.definition: An amount by which spending is greater than income.
cards.example: The department reduced its budget deficit.

cards.definition: A prediction about future business results; to predict.
cards.example: The sales forecast was revised upward.

cards.definition: The system or department that pays employees.
cards.example: Payroll must receive timesheets by noon Friday.

cards.definition: A record book or file of financial accounts.
cards.example: The accountant checked the ledger for errors.

cards.definition: Something valuable owned by a person or company.
cards.example: Company vehicles are listed as business assets.

cards.definition: A debt or financial obligation.
cards.example: The loan appears as a liability on the report.

cards.definition: To give official permission for payment or action.
cards.example: Only managers can authorize refunds over $500.

cards.definition: Money sent as payment.
cards.example: Please include the invoice number with your remittance.

cards.definition: To gradually reduce a debt by making regular payments over time.
cards.example: The company will amortize the loan over five years.

cards.definition: The ease with which assets can be turned into cash without losing value.
cards.example: The firm maintains strong liquidity to cover short-term expenses.

cards.definition: The movement of money into and out of a business.
cards.example: Positive cash flow allows the company to invest in new equipment.

cards.definition: The regular costs of running a business, such as rent and utilities.
cards.example: Reducing overhead is one way to improve profitability.

cards.definition: Total, before any deductions or taxes are taken out.
cards.example: The gross profit before expenses was higher than expected.

cards.definition: A detailed plan of a journey.
cards.example: The travel agency e-mailed her the full itinerary.

cards.definition: An arrangement to keep a seat, room, or table for someone.
cards.example: I made a hotel reservation for three nights.

cards.definition: A place to stay, such as a hotel room.
cards.example: Conference accommodation is available at a reduced rate.

cards.definition: The document that allows a passenger to board a plane.
cards.example: Please have your boarding pass ready at the gate.

cards.definition: The act of leaving, especially on a journey.
cards.example: The departure time was moved to 7:45 A.M.

cards.definition: The place someone is traveling to.
cards.example: Brookfield is a popular destination for business travelers.

cards.definition: A period of waiting because something is late.
cards.example: The flight delay was caused by the storm.

cards.definition: The bags a traveler carries.
cards.example: Guests arriving early may store their luggage at the front desk.

cards.definition: Going to a place and returning.
cards.example: A round-trip ticket is cheaper than two one-way fares.

cards.definition: The passage between rows of seats or shelves.
cards.example: He prefers an aisle seat on long flights.

cards.definition: A vehicle that makes short, regular trips between two places.
cards.example: The hotel shuttle runs to the convention center every 30 minutes.

cards.definition: The price of a ticket for transportation.
cards.example: Train fares increase during the holiday season.

cards.definition: The checkpoint where goods entering a country are inspected.
cards.example: Declare all purchases when you pass through customs.

cards.definition: A short stop between connecting flights.
cards.example: The itinerary includes a two-hour layover in Singapore.

cards.definition: To go somewhere with another person.
cards.example: A sales assistant will accompany the clients to the site.

cards.definition: To plan or organize something.
cards.example: The hotel can arrange airport transportation.

cards.definition: The process of registering at a hotel or airport.
cards.example: Hotel check-in begins at 3:00 P.M.

cards.definition: The process of leaving a hotel after paying.
cards.example: Check-out is by 11:00 A.M.

cards.definition: A hotel employee who helps guests with services and information.
cards.example: Ask the concierge to reserve a restaurant table.

cards.definition: A large formal meeting or event.
cards.example: The shuttle runs to the convention center.

cards.definition: A building where passengers arrive or depart.
cards.example: The flight leaves from Terminal 2.

cards.definition: To move from one vehicle or place to another.
cards.example: Passengers must transfer to a bus at Central Station.

cards.definition: The path used to travel from one place to another.
cards.example: The downtown route is faster during the morning.

cards.definition: Regular travel between home and work.
cards.example: Her daily commute takes about forty minutes.

cards.definition: A document that can be exchanged for a service.
cards.example: The airline issued a meal voucher after the delay.

cards.definition: A move to a better seat, room, or service.
cards.example: The hotel offered a room upgrade at no extra charge.

cards.definition: A useful or pleasant feature of a hotel or place.
cards.example: The rate includes several amenities, including breakfast.

cards.definition: The number or percentage of rooms being used.
cards.example: Hotel occupancy is highest during trade shows.

cards.definition: Visiting interesting places as a tourist.
cards.example: The itinerary leaves one afternoon for sightseeing.

cards.definition: The act of calling off a booking or event.
cards.example: Cancellations made seven days ahead receive a refund.

cards.definition: The exit at an airport where passengers board their plane.
cards.example: Flight 217 is now boarding at gate 14.

cards.definition: A small bag that a passenger takes onto a plane.
cards.example: Please place your carry-on in the overhead bin.

cards.definition: A short stop between flights, usually in a connecting city.
cards.example: We had a two-hour stopover in Singapore before our final flight.

cards.definition: A person whose job is to carry luggage at a hotel or station.
cards.example: The porter helped us with our bags at the hotel entrance.

cards.definition: A place where money of one country can be changed into another.
cards.example: I stopped at the currency exchange before the taxi ride.

cards.definition: A person who formally applies for a job or position.
cards.example: Applicants must submit two letters of recommendation.

cards.definition: A document summarizing a person's work history and skills.
cards.example: Send a resume and cover letter by April 30.

cards.definition: A formal meeting to assess a job candidate.

cards.definition: A skill, ability, or credential that makes someone suitable.

cards.definition: Something that is needed or demanded.

cards.definition: Knowledge or skill gained from doing a job.
cards.example: Insurance industry experience is preferred but not required.

cards.definition: To move someone to a higher position.

cards.definition: To stop working permanently, usually because of age.
cards.example: The mentoring tent is staffed by retired executives.

cards.definition: A fixed regular payment for work.
cards.example: The position offers a competitive salary.

cards.definition: Extras an employer provides, such as insurance or vacation.

cards.definition: The process of learning job skills.
cards.example: All representatives must complete the online training module.

cards.definition: A letter or statement supporting a candidate.
cards.example: Letters of recommendation must accompany the application.

cards.definition: A trial period for a new employee.
cards.example: New hires complete a three-month probation period.

cards.definition: All the people who work for a company or in a region.

cards.definition: To find people for jobs; a newly hired person.

cards.definition: To introduce a new employee to a company and job.

cards.definition: An available job or empty position.

cards.definition: A person being considered for a job or role.
cards.example: Each candidate will meet two supervisors.

cards.definition: A person who can describe a candidate's work or character.
cards.example: Applicants must provide two professional references.

cards.definition: Training that introduces a person to a new workplace.
cards.example: Orientation for new hires begins at 9 A.M.

cards.definition: Qualified or allowed to do or receive something.
cards.example: Part-time staff are eligible for the discount.

cards.definition: A formal review of performance or quality.
cards.example: Annual evaluations will be completed in May.

cards.definition: To organize and carry out an activity.
cards.example: The supervisor will conduct the interviews.

cards.definition: To officially leave a job.
cards.example: She resigned after accepting a position abroad.

cards.definition: To give someone a task or responsibility.
cards.example: The manager assigned the report to Jason.

cards.definition: A scheduled period of work.
cards.example: The evening shift begins at 4 P.M.

cards.definition: Time worked beyond regular hours.
cards.example: Overtime must be approved in advance.

cards.definition: An experienced person who advises someone; to advise.

cards.definition: To move to another position or location.
cards.example: Employees may request a transfer after one year.

cards.definition: Pay and benefits received for work.
cards.example: The compensation package includes health insurance.

cards.definition: The process of helping a new employee become familiar with a job.
cards.example: The onboarding program includes a tour and a week of training.

cards.definition: The rate at which employees leave a company and are replaced.

cards.definition: The act of giving someone a higher rank or position in a company.
cards.example: She received a promotion to senior manager last year.

cards.definition: The act of ending someone's employment, especially for poor performance.
cards.example: The company issued a written warning before the dismissal.

cards.definition: All the people employed in a particular company or industry.
cards.example: The company plans to expand its workforce by 10 percent.

cards.definition: A written promise to repair or replace a product if it fails.

cards.definition: A paper or digital proof of payment.

cards.definition: To buy something; the thing bought.

cards.definition: Goods that are bought and sold.

cards.definition: The complete stock of goods a business holds.

cards.definition: A quantity of goods sent together.

cards.definition: The act of bringing goods to a customer.

cards.definition: To return a product and receive another in its place.

cards.definition: Given free of charge.

cards.definition: A ticket or document that can be exchanged for goods or a discount.

cards.definition: A large quantity; buying in bulk means buying a lot at once.

cards.definition: Having a fault; not working correctly.

cards.definition: Not currently available for sale.

cards.definition: A list or book showing products for sale.

cards.definition: A passage between shelves or rows.

cards.definition: The place or process of paying for goods.

cards.definition: A business that sells goods directly to customers.

cards.definition: Selling goods in large quantities, often to businesses.

cards.definition: A company or person that provides goods.

cards.definition: A person or company that sells goods or services.

cards.definition: A stated price for goods or services; to state that price.

cards.definition: A fault or problem in a product.

cards.definition: Something provided in place of another item.

cards.definition: A partial refund after a purchase.
cards.example: Customers can request a rebate online.

cards.definition: A sale of goods at reduced prices to empty inventory.

cards.definition: A store or location where goods are sold.

cards.definition: To fill shelves again with products.

cards.definition: An order for an item not currently in stock.

cards.definition: To put parts together.

cards.definition: Strong and able to last a long time.

cards.definition: A small piece of paper that gives the holder a discount on a product.

cards.definition: A printed record showing that money has been paid for goods.

cards.definition: Money returned to a customer who is not satisfied with a purchase.

cards.definition: A special offer to increase sales; also a job advancement.

cards.definition: A set of questions used to gather opinions.

cards.definition: The people watching or listening to something.

cards.definition: A customer's continued support for a company.

cards.definition: A group or goal aimed at; to aim at that group.

cards.definition: Public attention or visibility.

cards.definition: Attention from the public or media.

cards.definition: A group of people with shared traits.
cards.example: The product appeals to a younger demographic.

cards.definition: A small area used by a vendor at an event.

cards.definition: A large sign used for advertising or information.
cards.example: The banner will hang above the registration desk.

cards.definition: An arrangement that shows products; to show.

cards.definition: Comments about how good or useful something is.

cards.definition: To publicly support or recommend something.

cards.definition: A small amount used to try something; to try.

cards.definition: To agree to receive a service or publication regularly.

cards.definition: The number of copies distributed.

cards.definition: An event held to collect money for a cause.

cards.definition: Meeting people to build professional relationships.

cards.definition: To introduce a new product or service to the public.

cards.definition: A planned series of activities designed to achieve a specific goal.

cards.definition: A person or company that pays for an event in exchange for advertising.

cards.definition: The specific group of people that an advertisement is designed to reach.

cards.definition: Work done to keep equipment or buildings in good condition.

cards.example: Only trained staff may operate the forklift.

cards.definition: To put parts together to make a product.
cards.example: Workers assemble the units on Line 2.

cards.definition: One part of a machine or product.
cards.example: A missing component delayed production.

cards.definition: Not working correctly because of a fault.
cards.example: Defective chargers will be replaced.

cards.definition: The process of checking that products meet standards.
cards.example: Quality control found two damaged parts.

cards.definition: A company that provides materials or products.
cards.example: The supplier confirmed next-day delivery.

cards.definition: To obtain goods or services, especially for business.
cards.example: The team procured new safety gloves.

cards.definition: The time between ordering and receiving something.
cards.example: The lead time for custom desks is four weeks.

cards.definition: Work or orders waiting to be completed.
cards.example: The shipping backlog was cleared by Friday.

cards.definition: To complete an order or requirement.
cards.example: The warehouse can fulfill the order today.

cards.definition: A lack of enough supply.
cards.example: A parts shortage slowed production.

cards.definition: More supply than is needed.
cards.example: Surplus inventory will be sold at a discount.

cards.definition: Easily broken or damaged.
cards.example: Mark fragile items before shipping.

cards.definition: Likely to spoil quickly.
cards.example: Perishable goods require refrigerated trucks.

cards.definition: A group of products made or handled together.
cards.example: The first batch passed inspection.

cards.definition: The amount produced by a worker, machine, or factory.
cards.example: Factory output increased in June.

cards.definition: Time when equipment is not operating.
cards.example: Regular service reduces machine downtime.

cards.definition: To make a process happen faster.
cards.example: The manager asked the carrier to expedite the shipment.

cards.definition: A group of goods sent for sale or delivery.
cards.example: The consignment arrived at the port Monday.

cards.definition: The government department that collects taxes on goods coming into a country.
cards.example: The shipment was held at customs for an extra inspection.

cards.definition: A unique code used to follow the progress of a shipped package.
cards.example: Please send me the tracking number so I can monitor the delivery.

cards.definition: Goods carried by ship, plane, or truck.
cards.example: The cargo arrived at the warehouse two days ahead of schedule.

cards.definition: To send something, especially goods or a vehicle, to a particular place.
cards.example: Two trucks were dispatched to the construction site this morning.

cards.definition: An arrangement to keep a room, table, or seat.
cards.example: The reservation is under Chen.

cards.definition: A person who welcomes visitors or guests.
cards.example: The receptionist confirmed the room number.

cards.definition: An available room or space.
cards.example: The hotel has no vacancies this weekend.

cards.definition: A set of connected hotel rooms.
cards.example: The executive suite includes a meeting area.

cards.definition: The entrance area of a hotel or building.
cards.example: Guests waited in the lobby.

cards.definition: An outdoor flat area for sitting or dining.
cards.example: The rooftop terrace is closed for a reception.

cards.definition: Providing food and drink for an event.
cards.example: Catering setup requires a cleaning fee.

cards.definition: A meal where people serve themselves from many dishes.
cards.example: Breakfast buffet ends at 9:30.

cards.definition: A drink.
cards.example: Complimentary beverages are served in the lounge.

cards.definition: A ticket that can be exchanged for a product or service.
cards.example: Guests received drink vouchers.

cards.definition: Provided free of charge.
cards.example: The room includes complimentary breakfast.

cards.definition: A useful feature or service for guests.
cards.example: Hotel amenities include a fitness center.

cards.definition: The hotel department that cleans rooms.
cards.example: Call housekeeping for extra towels.

cards.definition: A hotel employee who helps guests with arrangements.
cards.example: The concierge booked a city tour.

cards.definition: A formal large meal for many people.
cards.example: The awards banquet starts at 7 P.M.

cards.definition: A formal party or the place where guests check in.
cards.example: The private reception is on Thursday.

cards.definition: The number of rooms being used.
cards.example: Occupancy rises during conventions.

cards.definition: The standard price charged by a hotel or venue.
cards.example: The house rate includes wireless Internet.

cards.definition: The hotel desk where guests check in and ask questions.
cards.example: Leave your luggage at the front desk.

cards.definition: Permission to leave a hotel room after the normal time.
cards.example: Late checkout is available on request.

cards.definition: Permission to enter a hotel room before the normal time.
cards.example: Early check-in is subject to availability.

cards.definition: A hotel service that brings food to guest rooms.
cards.example: Room service is available until 10 P.M.

cards.definition: Sheets, towels, and similar cloth items.
cards.example: Fresh linens are delivered daily.

cards.definition: To provide what someone needs or has requested.
cards.example: The restaurant can accommodate large groups.

cards.definition: The place where an event is held.
cards.example: The hotel is a popular wedding venue.

cards.definition: The maximum number that can fit in a place.
cards.example: The hall has a capacity of 180 people.

cards.definition: A list of food and drinks available.
cards.example: The menu changes every season.

cards.definition: Related to food needs or restrictions.
cards.example: Please tell us about dietary requests.

cards.definition: Extra money given for service.
cards.example: A 15 percent gratuity is added to large parties.

cards.definition: To provide a place for an event; a person who welcomes guests.
cards.example: The chamber will host the business fair.

cards.definition: A desirable or useful feature of a place, such as a hotel.
cards.example: The hotel offers amenities such as a gym, pool, and free breakfast.

cards.definition: A set of connected rooms, especially in a hotel.
cards.example: The executive suite has a separate living area and a bedroom.

cards.definition: A hotel employee who helps guests with arrangements such as bookings.
cards.example: Ask the concierge to recommend a good restaurant nearby.
cards.vi: nhân viên lễ tân khách sạn

cards.definition: The department in a hotel responsible for cleaning rooms.
cards.example: Housekeeping will bring extra towels to your room within the hour.

cards.definition: The process of registering on arrival at a hotel or airport.
cards.example: Check-in begins at 3 P.M. on the day of arrival.

cards.definition: The process of leaving a hotel and paying the bill.
cards.example: Check-out is at noon, but late check-out can be arranged.

cards.definition: A request for information.
cards.example: Respond to every customer inquiry within one day.
cards.vi: câu hỏi, yêu cầu thông tin

cards.definition: A statement that something is wrong or unsatisfactory.
cards.example: The manager handled the complaint professionally.

cards.definition: To find an answer or solution to a problem.
cards.example: Customer service resolved the issue quickly.

cards.definition: To send a problem to someone with more authority.
cards.example: Escalate billing disputes to a supervisor.

cards.definition: A person who speaks or acts for a company.
cards.example: A representative will call you this afternoon.

cards.definition: Polite and respectful.
cards.example: The front-desk staff were courteous.

cards.definition: Done quickly or on time.
cards.example: Customers expect prompt replies.

cards.definition: To help someone.
cards.example: Staff will assist visitors at the entrance.

cards.definition: To make something easier to understand.
cards.example: Please clarify the return policy.

cards.definition: To say sorry for a problem.
cards.example: We apologize for the inconvenience.

cards.definition: Trouble or difficulty caused to someone.
cards.example: The notice thanked tenants for their patience during the inconvenience.

cards.definition: The feeling that expectations were met.
cards.example: The company tracks customer satisfaction.

cards.definition: Comments about a product or service.
cards.example: Your feedback helps us improve.

cards.definition: An official rule or way of doing something.
cards.example: The refund policy changed last month.

cards.definition: An official series of steps.
cards.example: Follow the new refund procedure.

cards.definition: A website used to access services or information.
cards.example: Refund requests must be entered in the portal.

cards.definition: The current state of something.
cards.example: Check the portal for the request status.

cards.definition: A recorded customer service request.
cards.example: Open a ticket for each technical issue.

cards.definition: An answer or reaction.
cards.example: The response was clear and helpful.

cards.definition: To contact someone again about a previous matter.
cards.example: Follow up with the client after delivery.

cards.definition: To provide a new item instead of a faulty one.
cards.example: The company will replace the damaged lamp.

cards.definition: To fix something; the act of fixing it.
cards.example: The warranty covers the repair.

cards.definition: Paid for before use.
cards.example: Use the prepaid label to mail the item.

cards.definition: A sticker or tag with information.
cards.example: Attach the return label to the box.

cards.definition: Money returned to a customer; to return money.
cards.example: The refund will appear within five business days.

cards.definition: A return of one item for another.
cards.example: The store allowed an exchange without a fee.

cards.definition: A promise that something will happen or work.
cards.example: The service guarantee covers late delivery.

cards.definition: The state of being ready or possible to use.
cards.example: Early check-in depends on room availability.

cards.definition: To tell someone officially.
cards.example: Notify your supervisor if you cannot attend.

cards.definition: To keep a customer, employee, or document.
cards.example: Good service helps retain customers.

cards.definition: A statement that something is wrong or not satisfactory.
cards.example: The customer filed a complaint about the delayed delivery.

cards.definition: Money returned to a customer who is not satisfied with a purchase.
cards.example: I would like to request a refund for this defective product.

cards.definition: To pass a problem to a person at a higher level for resolution.
cards.example: The agent escalated the issue to the technical support team.

cards.definition: The amount of time it takes to reply to a request or message.
cards.example: Our average response time is less than 24 hours.

cards.definition: A set of rules or a plan of action agreed upon by an organization.
cards.example: Our return policy allows exchanges within 30 days of purchase.
